heading: Child safety
label: 32304B Child safety
notes: References in TextThe date of enactment of this section, referred to in subsec. (b), is the date of enactment of , which was approved .Pub. L. 117–58Nov. 15, 2021

children.heading: .—Passenger motor vehicle
children.label: 1 .—Passenger motor vehicle
children.text: The term “passenger motor vehicle” has the meaning given that term in section 32101.

children.heading: .—Rear-designated seating position
children.label: 2 .—Rear-designated seating position
children.text: The term “rear-designated seating position” means designated seating positions that are rearward of the front seat.

children.heading: .—Secretary
children.label: 3 .—Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of Transportation.

children.heading: .—Rulemaking
children.label: b .—Rulemaking
children.text: Not later than 2 years after the date of enactment of this section, the Secretary shall issue a final rule requiring all new passenger motor vehicles weighing less than 10,000 pounds gross vehicle weight to be equipped with a system to alert the operator to check rear-designated seating positions after the vehicle engine or motor is deactivated by the operator.
shall include a distinct auditory and visual alert, which may be combined with a haptic alert; and
shall be activated when the vehicle motor is deactivated by the operator.

children.heading: .—Phase-in
children.label: d .—Phase-in
children.text: The rule issued pursuant to subsection (b) shall require full compliance with the rule beginning on September 1st of the first calendar year that begins 2 years after the date on which the final rule is issued.
